2603.19583.title: Skilled AI Agents for Embedded and IoT Systems Development
2603.19583.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) and agentic systems have shown promise for automated software development, but applying them to hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) embedded and Internet-of-Things (IoT) systems remains challenging due to the tight coupling between software logic and physical hardware behavior. Code that compiles successfully may still fail when deployed on real devices because of timing constraints, peripheral initialization requirements, or hardware-specific behaviors. To address this challenge, we introduce a skills-based agentic framework for HIL embedded development together with IoT-SkillsBench, a benchmark designed to systematically evaluate AI agents in real embedded programming environments. IoT-SkillsBench spans three representative embedded platforms, 23 peripherals, and 42 tasks across three difficulty levels, where each task is evaluated under three agent configurations (no-skills, LLM-generated skills, and human-expert skills) and validated through real hardware execution. Across 378 hardware validated experiments, we show that concise human-expert skills with structured expert knowledge enable near-perfect success rates across platforms.
2603.19583.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）和自主系統在自動化軟體開發方面顯示出潛力，但將它們應用於硬體在環（HIL）嵌入式和物聯網（IoT）系統仍然具有挑戰性，因為軟體邏輯與實體硬體行為之間緊密耦合。成功編譯的程式碼在實際設備上部署時仍可能失敗，這是由於時間限制、外圍設備初始化要求或硬體特定行為所致。為了解決這一挑戰，我們引入了一個基於技能的自主框架，用於HIL嵌入式開發，並推出IoT-SkillsBench，這是一個旨在系統性評估AI代理在真實嵌入式程式設計環境中的基準。IoT-SkillsBench涵蓋三個具有代表性的嵌入式平台、23個外圍設備和42個任務，分為三個難度級別，每個任務在三種代理配置（無技能、LLM生成技能和人類專家技能）下進行評估，並通過實際硬體執行進行驗證。在378個硬體驗證實驗中，我們顯示出結構化專家知識的簡潔人類專家技能使得各平台的成功率接近完美。
2603.19583.author: Yiming Li et.al.
2603.19583.authors: Yiming Li, Yuhan Cheng, Mingchen Ma, Yihang Zou, Ningyuan Yang, Wei Cheng, Hai "Helen" Li, Yiran Chen, Tingjun Chen
